examples.title: Virtual Circuit Data Transfer
examples.description: Demonstrates how data is transferred in Virtual Circuit switching using VCI
examples.explanation: In VC switching, each router maintains a VCI table. When a packet arrives, the router looks up the incoming VCI, replaces it with the outgoing VCI (label swapping), and forwards the packet.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(N) where N is number of active VCs

examples.title: Datagram Packet Forwarding
examples.description: Shows how each datagram packet is independently routed at each router
examples.explanation: In datagram switching, each packet carries the full destination IP address. Each router performs an independent routing lookup for every packet. The address remains unchanged throughout the journey.
examples.timeComplexity: O(log R) where R is routing table entries

examples.title: VC Connection Setup Simulation
examples.description: Python simulation of Virtual Circuit connection establishment
examples.code:
class VirtualCircuit:
    def __init__(self, vc_id, path):
        self.vc_id = vc_id
        self.path = path  # List of routers in the path
        self.state = "IDLE"
    
    def establish(self):
        """Phase 1: Establish virtual circuit"""
        print(f"[VC {self.vc_id}] Initiating connection setup...")
        for router in self.path:
            # Reserve resources at each router
            router.reserve_resources(self.vc_id)
        self.state = "ESTABLISHED"
        print(f"[VC {self.vc_id}] Connection established successfully!")
    
    def transfer_data(self, data_packets):
        """Phase 2: Transfer data with VCI swapping"""
        if self.state != "ESTABLISHED":
            print("Error: VC not established!")
            return
            
        print(f"[VC {self.vc_id}] Transferring {len(data_packets)} packets...")
        current_vci = 10  # Starting VCI
        
        for i, packet in enumerate(data_packets):
            print(f"  Packet {i+1}: ", end="")
            for router in self.path:
                # VCI swapping at each router
                next_vci = router.swap_vci(current_vci)
                print(f"Router{router.id}: VCI {current_vci}→{next_vci} ", end="")
                current_vci = next_vci
            print("→ Destination")
    
    def teardown(self):
        """Phase 3: Release resources"""
        print(f"[VC {self.vc_id}] Tearing down connection...")
        for router in self.path:
            router.release_resources(self.vc_id)
        self.state = "IDLE"
        print(f"[VC {self.vc_id}] Connection terminated.")

# Example usage
# vc = VirtualCircuit("VC-1", [router1, router2, router3])
# vc.establish()
# vc.transfer_data(["data1", "data2", "data3"])
# vc.teardown()
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates the three phases of Virtual Circuit switching: establishment (resource reservation), data transfer (VCI swapping at each router), and teardown (resource release).
examples.timeComplexity: O(P × R) where P = packets, R = routers in path

examples.title: Datagram Packet Processing
examples.description: C code demonstrating independent packet routing in datagram networks
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates that in datagram switching, each packet carries the full source and destination IP addresses. Each router performs an independent routing lookup for every packet without referencing any connection state.
examples.timeComplexity: O(R) per packet where R = routing table entries
